canonicalization_vectors.description: APS §4.1 action_ref preimage (4 string/array-of-string fields; no numbers, so the ±(2^53-1) integer bound is moot — restricted subset == full RFC 8785 here)
canonicalization_vectors.name: canon_action_ref_aps

canonicalization_vectors.description: Non-ASCII strings MUST be raw UTF-8, never \uXXXX-escaped (§4.3.4). Mix of Latin-1, CJK, and emoji to exercise multi-byte UTF-8.
canonicalization_vectors.name: canon_non_ascii_strings

canonicalization_vectors.description: Canonicalisation is pure serialisation (RFC 8785) and does NOT apply Unicode normalisation: a decomposed string is emitted byte-for-byte as given, NOT folded to NFC. The value is U+0041 U+030A (LATIN A + COMBINING RING) — distinct bytes from the precomposed U+00C5 'Å'. Interop note: NFC is a *producer* responsibility (e.g. APS sorts/normalises scopeRequired before canonicalising); a canonicaliser that silently NFC-folds here would diverge on this input.
canonicalization_vectors.name: canon_non_nfc_passthrough

canonicalization_vectors.description: Object keys MUST be ordered by UTF-16 code unit (§4.3.1, RFC 8785 §3.2.3), NOT by Unicode code point. The key '\uE000' (BMP private-use) vs '\U0001F511' (🔑, non-BMP) is the discriminating case: by code point \uE000 (57344) < 🔑 (128273) so \uE000 sorts first; by UTF-16 the 🔑 surrogate lead unit 0xD83D (55357) < 0xE000 so 🔑 sorts first. A code-point sort (naive Python/Go/Rust) and a UTF-16 sort (JS/.NET) disagree here — this vector forces all implementations onto UTF-16.
canonicalization_vectors.name: canon_non_bmp_key_ordering

canonicalization_vectors.description: Safe-integer boundary 2^53-1 = 9007199254740991 (§4.3.6). This value round-trips byte-identically everywhere; 2^53 (9007199254740992) is out of the signed-material domain and MUST be rejected at sign/verify.
canonicalization_vectors.name: canon_boundary_integer

canonicalization_vectors.description: C0 control characters in string values MUST use the two-char short escapes \b \f \n \r \t where defined, and \u00XX (lowercase hex) for every other code point < U+0020 (§4.3.4). '/' is NOT escaped; '"' and '\\' are. '<' '>' '&' are passed through literally (NOT HTML-escaped). The five reference encoders are hand-aligned on this; this vector pins it so a port that emits \u0008 for backspace, uppercase hex, or HTML-escapes diverges loudly instead of silently.

input_object.ctl:
a b	c
de fgh/i"j\k

canonicalization_vectors.name: canon_control_char_escaping

invalid_canonicalization_vectors.description: 2^53 = 9007199254740992 is one past the safe-integer boundary (§4.3.6); MUST be rejected at sign and verify in every implementation.
invalid_canonicalization_vectors.name: invalid_integer_above_safe_range

invalid_canonicalization_vectors.description: -(2^53) = -9007199254740992 is one past the negative boundary (§4.3.6); MUST be rejected.
invalid_canonicalization_vectors.name: invalid_integer_below_safe_range

invalid_canonicalization_vectors.description: Non-integer numbers (floats) MUST be rejected, not serialised (§4.3.5): cross-implementation float canonicalisation is out of scope for v1.
invalid_canonicalization_vectors.name: invalid_float

invalid_canonicalization_vectors.description: An unpaired UTF-16 surrogate (here a lone HIGH surrogate U+D800 in a string value) MUST be rejected at sign/verify (§4.3.4). It cannot be encoded to UTF-8 and the ports otherwise disagree: Python and Rust reject, Go and .NET substitute U+FFFD, and a JS JSON.stringify emits a \udXXX escape — a silent cross-implementation byte divergence. Rejection is the only portable contract.
invalid_canonicalization_vectors.name: invalid_lone_surrogate

invalid_canonicalization_vectors.description: A lone LOW surrogate U+DC00 (not preceded by a high) in a string value MUST be rejected (§4.3.4), same contract as a lone high surrogate. Distinct code path from the high-surrogate case in every port.
invalid_canonicalization_vectors.name: invalid_lone_surrogate_low

invalid_canonicalization_vectors.description: A lone surrogate U+D800 in an OBJECT KEY (not a value) MUST be rejected (§4.3.4): the rule applies to keys and values alike. Exercises the key-validation path, which is separate from value validation in the Python/TypeScript ports.
invalid_canonicalization_vectors.name: invalid_lone_surrogate_key

vectors.description: Minimal root receipt for a user_input operation
vectors.name: user_input_minimal

vectors.description: model_call citing the user_input as parent; inherits taint
vectors.name: model_call_inheriting_taint

vectors.description: tool_call hashing structured input args and output objects
vectors.name: tool_call_with_structured_args

vectors.description: sanitisation operation explicitly removing the external_user taint
vectors.name: sanitisation_removes_tag

vectors.description: guardrail_scan emitting a CLEAN verdict with ruleset hash
vectors.name: guardrail_scan_clean
